summary: Validated GitHub Actions workflow fix (Node 20 -> 22, removed yarn cache block) and verified static CRA build produces a self-contained, API-free portfolio bundle suitable for GitHub Pages.

validations_performed.cache_block_removed: PASS - setup-node 'with' keys = ['node-version'] only; no 'cache' or 'cache-dependency-path'
validations_performed.build_env: PASS - REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL='' and CI=false set on Build step
validations_performed.deploy_job: PASS - separate deploy job using actions/deploy-pages@v4 with github-pages environment
validations_performed.local_build: PASS - `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL='' CI=false yarn build` exit 0 in 17.97s; 'Compiled successfully'
validations_performed.no_api_references_in_bundle: PASS - grep -oE '/api/(chat|contact|resume/download|[a-zA-Z_]+)' on build/static/js/main.*.js returned ZERO matches; site is fully static

critical_code_review_comments: Workflow is clean and minimal. Optional (non-blocking) enhancement: add `actions/cache@v4` keyed on hashFiles('frontend/yarn.lock') to restore yarn caching without the buggy setup-node cache-dependency-path resolution. Not required - current workflow is correct.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is now a static UI-only portfolio deployed via GitHub Pages. No backend/MongoDB/LLM in scope. All future tests should validate either (a) the deploy.yml workflow or (b) the CRA build output under /app/frontend/build. Do NOT attempt API or playwright tests against backend endpoints - none exist for this deployment target.
rca of the issue: Original failure had two causes: (1) actions/setup-node@v4 default Node 20 is being deprecated on GitHub-hosted runners (Sept 2025 changelog) - fixed by bumping to node-version: 22 (current LTS). (2) The `cache: yarn` + `cache-dependency-path: frontend/yarn.lock` block on setup-node failed because setup-node resolves cache-dependency-path relative to the repo root and the resolver flagged the path as unresolvable in some runner contexts, producing 'No existing directories found' warning followed by the fatal 'Some specified paths were not resolved, unable to cache dependencies.' Removing the cache block eliminates the failure entirely; caching is purely an optimization. Both fixes verified: YAML parses, build runs end-to-end locally, output contains no /api references and uses relative asset paths.
